### `id`

integer

### `entry_number`

integer

# on the PACER docket page. For appellate cases, this may be the internal PACER ID for the document, when an entry ID is otherwise unavailable.

Lookups: `gt`, `gte`, `isnull`, `lt`, `lte`, `range`

### `date_created`

string (date-time)

The moment when the item was created.

Lookups: `day`, `gt`, `gte`, `hour`, `lt`, `lte`, `minute`, `month`, `range`, `second`, `year`

### `date_modified`

string (date-time)

The last moment when the item was modified. A value in year 1750 indicates the value is unknown

Lookups: `day`, `gt`, `gte`, `hour`, `lt`, `lte`, `minute`, `month`, `range`, `second`, `year`

### `date_filed`

string (date)

The created date of the Docket Entry according to the court timezone.

Lookups: `day`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `month`, `range`, `year`

### `pacer_sequence_number`

integer

The de_seqno value pulled out of dockets, RSS feeds, and sundry other pages in PACER. The place to find this is currently in the onclick attribute of the links in PACER. Because we do not have this value for all items in the DB, we do not use this value for anything. Still, we collect it for good measure.

Lookups: `gt`, `gte`, `isnull`, `lt`, `lte`, `range`
